title: As I Lay Dying
author: William Faulkner
content: “It’s not so much what a man says as the way he says it.”
action: Audit your communication for clarity and intent.

title: East of Eden
author: John Steinbeck
content: “Now that you don't have to be perfect, you can be good.”
action: Abandon perfectionism to focus on progress.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
content: “I'm standing on the edge of some crazy cliff. What I have to do, I have to catch everybody if they start to go over the cliff.”
action: Identify the high-risk points in your team's workflow.

title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
author: Zora Neale Hurston
content: “There are years that ask questions and years that answer.”
action: Switch from gathering information to decisive execution.

title: Death of a Salesman
author: Arthur Miller
content: “You can't eat the orange and throw the peel away—a man is not a piece of fruit!”
action: Value long-term sustainability over short-term gain.
